goal.name: Build KDT OS Into My Personal Operating System
goal.why: Use software to make fitness progress visible and repeatable.

project.name: KDT OS
project.purpose: Build a self-tracking tool for workouts and progress.
project.source_note: This project was selected from tracked uploaded projects.

problem_summary: I want KDT OS to analyze local project folders, detect languages, frameworks, skills, project health, and changes over time so I don't have to manually explain every project.

tool_decision.why_this_stack: A browser app is the fastest MVP and can be used on phone before building a native app.

bottleneck.why_it_blocks_progress: A tracker is not useful if workouts disappear after refresh.
bottleneck.next_smallest_action: Save one workout entry to localStorage or SQLite.

redundancy_review.keep_separate_reason: Keep separate only if the purpose is different from existing tracked work.

required_skills.why_needed: The app needs buttons and forms.

quest_plan.title: Build a Saved Workout Form
quest_plan.why: This solves the core tracker blocker: saving progress.
quest_plan.what_to_build: A browser page where you enter exercise, sets, reps, and save it so it remains after refresh.

proof_strategy: Proof must show data persists after refresh
Upload ZIP and screenshot of saved workout list
return_rule: Complete one quest, submit proof, update skill evidence, then return to this path and choose the next quest.
decision_engine_note: KDT OS repaired or filled this path using the Universal Decision Engine when AI output was vague.

trust.rule: KDT OS must never treat an inference or suggestion as a fact.

project.source_note: This project was selected from tracked uploaded projects.

known_vs_inferred: Facts come from uploaded/analyzed projects or direct user confirmation.
Inferences come from AI reasoning and require review.
Suggestions are ideas only until confirmed.
